{{Infobox technique
| name = Striker technique
| introduced = cradle-02-soulsmith
}}

''See also: [[Striker technique Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|A [[Striker]] technique is a method of combat used by [[sacred artists]] to project power outward, typically through individual attacks that carry significant force behind a single motion. These techniques are characterized by having a clear input and output, often appearing as elongated or traveling projectiles such as beams of light, fireballs, needles, or elemental shapes like snakes and birds. While they can be used to project [[madra]] over distances—ranging from a few inches to hundreds of yards—they are generally slower and less powerful than [[Enforcer techniques]], which focus on enhancing the user's physical movements. [[Striker techniques]] can be highly diverse, manifesting as simple pulses of [[madra]] or complex, coordinated volleys that resemble swarms or symphonies. Advanced users can imbue these attacks with their own will, allowing the techniques to act with a level of autonomy similar to spirits. They can be integrated with various elements, such as shadow, blood, lightning, or force, and can even be combined with sacred instruments or specific [[Paths]] to increase their lethality. In combat, they are often used to disrupt an opponent's madra channels, force defensive reactions, or deliver decisive blows from a distance.}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|A [[Striker technique]] can hiss and spit with green fury.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9">''Soulsmith'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Striker techniques]] are combat methods utilized by [[Jai Daishou]]. During a confrontation, [[Yerin]] employs one of these techniques, which is described as [[dragon]] breath. [[Lindon]] later attempts to practice a version of the technique by imitating a [[dragon]]'s breath. Eventually, [[Lindon]] successfully uses a [[Striker]] technique himself.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] uses a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13">''Blackflame'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Striker technique]] is described as [[dragon]] breath.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c15">''Blackflame'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Striker technique]] [[Lindon]] is practicing is an imitation of a [[dragon’s breath]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c16">''Blackflame'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Striker techniques]] are combat methods used by [[Jai Daishou]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c18">''Blackflame'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] used a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c19">''Blackflame'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|A [[Striker technique]] can manifest as a beam of white light.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c6">''Skysworn'', chapter 6</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Striker]] technique is a specific type of technique utilized by Enkai and [[Ekeri]], as well as one that was kindled by Sophara. During his journey, [[Lindon]]'s arm devours this technique, which is ultimately located within his white arm. This technique manifests as a thin shadow-blade and can be mimicked when [[Lindon]] pushes [[Dross]] out of his core as if he were releasing it.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Striker technique]] is a type of technique used by Enkai.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c1">''Ghostwater'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Striker technique]] is a technique that [[Lindon]]'s arm devours.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c10">''Ghostwater'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Ekeri]] uses a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|Sophara kindled a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Lindon]] pushed [[Dross]] out of his core like he was releasing a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c15">''Ghostwater'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The thin shadow-blade was a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18">''Ghostwater'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Striker technique]] is located in [[Lindon's white arm]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c18" />}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Truegold]] used a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9">''Underlord'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A [[Striker technique]] was used to follow up the [[Ruler technique]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c11">''Underlord'', chapter 11</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Striker techniques]] are combat methods that function by projecting [[madra]]. These attacks possess enough power to tear a person in half, and they can be successfully landed even from the top of a pillar. While such techniques can be caught by a bronze plate or deflected by [[Yerin]], they are also components of more complex layering techniques. Such advanced maneuvers may require the simultaneous use of a [[Forger technique]], a [[Striker]] technique, and a [[Ruler technique]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Striker techniques]] involve projecting [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c5">''Uncrowned'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A bronze plate emerged to catch the [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8">''Uncrowned'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A [[Striker technique]] can tear a person in half.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c13">''Uncrowned'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Yerin]] deflects [[Striker techniques]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c14">''Uncrowned'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A [[Striker technique]] can be landed from the top of a pillar.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c17">''Uncrowned'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Layering techniques may require using a [[Ruler technique]], [[Striker technique]], and [[Forger technique]] simultaneously.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Striker]] is a specific category of technique that can be imbued with a living will. While these techniques are utilized by enemies during battle and by [[Overlords]] to intervene in duels, they present a risk where attackers may hit one another if used improperly. During the conflict, [[Yan Shoumei]] employed a red needle [[Striker]] technique, and both the [[Blood Sage]] and an unnamed assailant launched techniques toward [[Yerin]]. Although [[Yerin]] possesses a [[Striker]] technique, the [[Winter Sage]] deems her version acceptable but not worth further development. [[Eithan]] is able to counter these attacks, as his [[Hollow King’s Mantle]] can hurl a [[Striker]] technique back at its source.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The attackers cannot use [[Striker techniques]] without hitting each other.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1">''Wintersteel'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Striker]] is a type of technique.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Yerin]]'s [[Striker technique]] is considered fine but not worth working on by the [[Winter Sage]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Striker techniques]] can be imbued with a living will.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Striker technique]] was blasted toward [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Overlords]] would use [[Striker techniques]] to intervene in duels.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Striker techniques]] were used by enemies during the battle.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Yan Shoumei]] used a red needle [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c22">''Wintersteel'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Eithan]]'s [[Hollow King’s Mantle]] can hurl a [[Striker technique]] back.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27">''Wintersteel'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Blood Sage]] launched a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c27" />}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Striker techniques]] are combat abilities that can manifest in various forms, such as a streaking line of golden madra or [[Jai Long]]'s white snake of [[madra]]. These techniques can be used to push targets with force or inflict the illusion of pain upon an opponent. During various conflicts, these abilities have been deployed by security constructs, the Matriarch, and bystanders targeting [[Eithan]] and his house. Finally, a [[Titan]] utilizes a [[Striker]] technique against [[Lindon]] that appears as a burst of bright golden light.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The security constructs kindle their [[Striker techniques]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Striker technique]] can manifest as a streaking line of golden madra.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|One of the girl's [[sword]]-arms flicked a [[Striker technique]] out of the air.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7">''Bloodline'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jai Long]]'s [[Striker technique]] manifests as a white snake of [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Striker techniques]] can be used to push targets with force.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Striker technique]] can be used to inflict the illusion of pain.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The Matriarch used a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13">''Bloodline'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Striker techniques]] were used by bystanders against the house and [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Titan]] is going to use a [[Striker technique]] against [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Titan]] uses a [[Striker technique]] that appears as bright golden light.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c20">''Bloodline'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Striker techniques]] are typically characterized by an elongated structure featuring distinct inputs and outputs. These techniques produce [[madra]] capable of washing over existing barriers, a property demonstrated when [[Eithan]] utilized a miniature version of the method. While [[Reigan Shen]] employed an orange-colored technique, [[Ozmanthus]] showed that these abilities can be dismantled, as seen during his interaction with a prisoner.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] used a tiny [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8">''Reaper'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Striker techniques]] are usually elongated with clear input and output.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c12">''Reaper'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Striker technique]] produces [[madra]] that can wash over barriers.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozmanthus]] demonstrates dismantling a prisoner's [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c14">''Reaper'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]] used an orange [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c17">''Reaper'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The lightning eagle gathered a powerful [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Red Faith]] uses a [[Striker technique]] layered over an [[Enforcer technique]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c11">''Dreadgod'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Striker technique]] of death and destruction can cause a colorless slash.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[The Fleshripper]] released a [[Striker technique]] that split into twelve projectiles.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|During the unfolding conflicts, [[Reigan Shen]] unleashed a rapid barrage of [[Striker techniques]]. [[Northstrider]] later employed a technique manifesting as a crimson serpent of [[blood madra]] with white edges. As the battles progressed, [[Calling Storms]] prepared a [[Striker]] technique for use, while [[Orthos]] demonstrated immense power by crushing such techniques in his hands. In a significant moment, [[Lindon]] consumed a [[Striker]] technique taken from the Dragon, and the [[Wandering Titan]] also utilized a [[Striker]] technique.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Reigan Shen]] unleashes a barrage of [[Striker techniques]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5">''Waybound'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Northstrider]] used a [[Striker technique]] that was a white-edged crimson serpent of [[blood madra]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Calling Storms]] has a [[Striker technique]] prepared.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c14">''Waybound'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Orthos]] crushed [[Striker techniques]] in his hands.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19">''Waybound'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] consumes a [[Striker technique]] from the Dragon.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Wandering Titan]] uses a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Yan Shoumei]] can conjure [[blood madra]] into a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c3">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fury]] uses a [[Striker technique]] by gathering [[madra]] in his hands.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c6">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 6</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Techniques]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
